title: CleanSpark's (CLSK's) Bitcoin Mining Feeds AI Infrastructure Business
text: CleanSpark (NASDAQ: CLSK) operates a dual-revenue model combining cryptocurrency mining with data center services. The company leverages profits from Bitcoin production to fund infrastructure expansion, which then supports both mining operations and commercial clients. In March 2026, the company mined 658 Bitcoin, producing an average of 21.24 BTC daily. By quarter's end, CleanSpark's total Bitcoin holdings reached 13,561 coins after generating 1,799 BTC in Q1 2026. CleanSpark closed its second Texas data center acquisition in March 2026, adding 300 megawatts of power capacity. This brought contracted capacity to 1.8 gigawatts, with 808 megawatts dedicated to mining operations. As CEO Matt Schultz explained, the business model creates a virtuous cycle: The Bitcoin mining business generates cash flow, and the company uses this cash flow to develop its infrastructure capacity. Expanding infrastructure enables increased mining scale while monetizing excess capacity through AI and cloud computing clients. Based in Nevada, CleanSpark prioritizes clean energy utilization across its operations, distinguishing itself in the energy-intensive mining sector.
